Evidence: deployed local Sinex/Sinnix fixes, restarted sinexd.service, runtime health healthy=true/serving=true with DB/NATS/raw-ingest DLQ/confirmation/replay/SSE healthy; raw consumer caught up pending=0 ack_pending=0 redelivered=0; core.events Timescale chunk estimate 80,872,976 rows, matching/exceeding the pre-incident ~74M expectation and consistent with the earlier exact restore count around 80.8M; source_material_registry exact 34,782; persisted DLQ exact 0. Residual broad recall latency is tracked separately as sinex-0tj; source-material debt/defrag remains under existing robustness work.

Implemented zero-event self-observation slice-timeout recovery. Timed-out self-observation materials with parsed_event_count=0 are now marked recovered_partial with dlq_policy=suppressed_zero_event_self_observation_timeout before raw DLQ routing; remediation planning classifies the recovery reason as low-severity acknowledgeable residue. Verification: xtask test -p sinexd -E 'test(stale_zero_event_self_observation_timeout_recovers_partial_without_dlq) | test(remediation_decision_acknowledges_empty_self_observation_timeout) | test(remediation_candidate_uses_recovery_reason_for_recovered_partial)' passed as job 2001356, 3 passed; xtask check -p sinexd passed as job 2001357; production health recheck healthy=true and raw-ingest DLQ empty.

Completed by c37b533b9. Zero-event slice_arrival_timeout materials now bypass raw-ingest DLQ and land as recovered_partial source-material remediation with material/source/count metadata; self-observation residue is low-severity acknowledgement, ordinary source-material residue is medium-severity inspection. Operator acknowledgement is through the existing safe per-material command sinexctl sources annotate <material_id> --tag acknowledged-empty-timeout. Verification: material-assembler focused job 2001359 passed 2 tests; remediation handler job 2001361 passed 4 tests; xtask check -p sinexd passed job 2001362 with no current sinexd diagnostics.

Implemented in flake.nix: launcher-only xtask --bg requests now skip pre-exec SQLx bootstrap and enter the coordinator first; the actual foreground/background child still bootstraps SQLx under a wrapper-level flock at .sinex/state/xtask-sqlx-bootstrap.lock. Implemented in working tree and verified before commit: zero-event ordinary orphaned sensing materials now mark recovered_partial with recovery_reason orphaned_zero_event_source_material_recovered_partial and dlq_policy suppressed_zero_event_source_material_restart_orphan, while eventful ordinary orphans keep explicit failure routing. API remediation_plan maps that recovery reason to inspect_empty_orphaned_source_material.
